content: I'm so excited about our new project at BioGenesis Pharma. We're working on a new drug development pipeline and I'm leading the data science team.
That sounds like a great opportunity. What's the goal of the project and what's your approach to the data science aspect?

Our goal is to develop a new treatment for a rare disease and we're using machine learning algorithms to analyze the data. I've been working with our team to develop a predictive model that can help us identify potential candidates for the treatment.
That sounds like a challenging but rewarding project. Have you considered collaborating with other teams or organizations to get additional expertise or resources?
Yes, we're actually collaborating with a team from Stanford University. They have a lot of experience with machine learning and they're helping us develop the predictive model.
I'm also getting married next year and my fiancé, Michael, is actually a data scientist too. He's been helping me with the project and it's been great to have his input.
That's great to hear. It's always helpful to have a second opinion, especially from someone with experience. What's the timeline for the project and what are the key milestones?
We need to finish the predictive model by the end of April and then we'll start testing it. We're planning to present our findings at a conference in June.

That's a tight deadline, but it sounds like you have a good plan in place. Don't forget to take some time for yourself, too - planning a wedding can be stressful. Maybe you can take a break and go for a mountain bike ride or work on your leatherworking project?

Yeah, I love mountain biking. I've actually been trying to get back into it after a few months of not riding. I've been going to the trails in Marin County and it's been great to get some exercise and fresh air.
That sounds like a great way to relax. Marin County is a beautiful place to ride. Maybe you can also share some photos of your leatherworking project? I'm sure it's coming along well.
I'd love to. I've actually been working on a new handbag design and it's been a fun project. I've been using a lot of different techniques and materials to create a unique look.
That sounds like a great project. I'm sure it'll turn out well. Don't forget to enjoy the process and take time to appreciate the little things, too.
I will, thanks for the reminder. I'm also thinking of taking a break from work and going on a trip to Yellowstone with Michael after the wedding.
That sounds like a great idea. You deserve a break and it's always nice to spend time with loved ones. What are you most looking forward to seeing or doing on the trip?
